ucs.title: [LLM] APT32 / OceanLotus PubPrn.vbs signed-script-proxy execution (script: moniker)
ucs.description: Hunts abuse of the Microsoft-signed pubprn.vbs script (T1216.001) invoked with a remote `script:` moniker URL — a LOLBAS technique most-publicly tied to APT32/OceanLotus (FireEye 2017, Volexity 2019) for AppLocker/SRP bypass when staging KerrDown/Cobalt Strike payloads. Outside of OceanLotus this technique sees almost zero benign use.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="cscript.exe" OR Processes.process_name="wscript.exe") AND Processes.process="*pubprn*" AND (Processes.process="*script:http*" OR Processes.process="*script:\\\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | regex cmdline="(?i)pubprn(\.vbs)?.*script:(https?://|\\\\)" | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("cscript.exe", "wscript.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has "pubprn"
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)script:(https?://|\\\\)"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent      = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ScriptHost  = FileName,
          CommandLine = ProcessCommandLine,
          FolderPath, SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "cscript.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "wscript.exe"
| where CommandLine has "pubprn"
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)script:(https?://|\\\\)"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT32 OceanLotus PubPrn.vbs Signed Script Proxy Execution
id: 9f8a47b2-3d6e-4a7c-9c1b-6e2a5b1c4f01
status: experimental
description: Detects pubprn.vbs invoked with a remote script: moniker URL or UNC path, a signed-script-proxy bypass attributed to APT32 / OceanLotus.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1216/001/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0050/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1216.001
  - attack.g0050
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
  selection_cmd_pubprn:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'pubprn'
  selection_cmd_remote:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'script:http'
      - 'script:\\'
  condition: selection_proc and selection_cmd_pubprn and selection_cmd_remote
falsepositives:
  - Extremely rare; legitimate printer publishing scripts do not use the script: moniker with remote URLs.
level: high
ucs.rationale: PubPrn.vbs is a Microsoft-signed VBS script (Printing_Admin_Scripts) that accepts a `script:` parameter — when paired with an HTTP(S) URL or UNC path, the Windows Script Host fetches and executes a remote scriptlet (.sct) under a signed-binary trust context. APT32 publicly pioneered this bypass against AppLocker for KerrDown/Cobalt Strike loader staging. The technique has near-zero benign baseline
_sigma_issues:
yaml/sigma error: mapping values are not allowed in this context
  in "<unicode string>", line 4, column 61

ucs.title: [LLM] APT32 KerrDown delivery chain: Office macro -> WSH/MSHTA with HTTP-staging or base64
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical OceanLotus initial-access -> execution chain (T1204.002 -> T1059.005/.007): a maldoc spear-phish opens in WinWord/Excel/PowerPoint/Outlook and the macro spawns wscript.exe / cscript.exe / mshta.exe with an HTTP staging URL, FromBase64String blob, ActiveXObject, or WScript.Shell call — the documented loader pattern for KerrDown, Denis backdoor and CS Beacon shellcode (Unit42, ESET, Cybereason "Cobalt Kitty").
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_id) as pid from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","WINWORD.EXE","EXCEL.EXE") AND Processes.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe") AND (Processes.process="*http://*" OR Processes.process="*https://*" OR Processes.process="*FromBase64String*" OR Processes.process="*ActiveXObject*" OR Processes.process="*WScript.Shell*" OR Processes.process="*GetObject*winmgmts*" OR Processes.process="*MSXML2.XMLHTTP*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","FromBase64String","ActiveXObject","WScript.Shell","MSXML2.XMLHTTP","GetObject")
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{120,}\b"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          OfficeParent     = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          OfficeParentCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ScriptHost       = FileName,
          ScriptCmd        = ProcessCommandLine,
          FolderPath, SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where ParentProcessName has_any ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe")
| where NewProcessName has_any ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe")
| where CommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","FromBase64String","ActiveXObject","WScript.Shell","MSXML2.XMLHTTP","GetObject")
   or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{120,}\b"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT32 OceanLotus Office Macro to WSH/MSHTA Loader Chain
id: 1c4b3d5e-7a2f-49b8-8d3e-2f4a6c1b9e02
status: experimental
description: Detects an Office application (Word/Excel/PowerPoint/Outlook) spawning a Windows Script Host or mshta with HTTP-staging, base64, or COM-automation indicators - the canonical APT32/OceanLotus KerrDown delivery chain.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0050/
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/tracking-oceanlotus-new-downloader-kerrdown/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1059.005
  - attack.t1059.007
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.g0050
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
  child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
  cmd_indicators:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - 'ActiveXObject'
      - 'WScript.Shell'
      - 'MSXML2.XMLHTTP'
      - 'GetObject'
  condition: parent and child and cmd_indicators
falsepositives:
  - Legacy line-of-business macros that legitimately call remote endpoints or COM automation - baseline before alerting.
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT32 (OceanLotus / Cobalt Kitty / BISMUTH) is one of the most prolific abusers of Office-macro -> WSH spawn chains, documented across Unit42's KerrDown reporting, ESET's PhantomNet/Denis analysis and Cybereason's Cobalt Kitty op. The combination is the actor's signature: Vietnamese-language phishing lures targeting ASEAN govts/auto/dissidents drop a maldoc whose VBA invokes wscript.exe or mshta.e

_field_issues.message: `Za` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `Za` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
